headline: Inference optimization and a cluster of serving techniques broke out as OpenAI filed confidentially for an IPO behind Anthropic.
body: A cluster of inference and serving techniques moved together this week, with inference optimization, parallel execution, attribution, and filter propagation all breaking out alongside new entrants resource analysis, AI engineering, percolation, and throughput scaling. On the corporate side, OpenAI filed confidentially for an IPO, following Anthropic's similar filing about a week earlier, escalating the competitive dynamic between the two [9][10]. Apple drew sustained attention from WWDC 2026, where it leaned on vision LLMs and Private Cloud Compute for Siri, shipped a Core AI library for running PyTorch models on its hardware, and pitched cheaper AI to attract smaller developers [3][5][11]. A New York Times account of researchers scooped by an AI on a years-long math problem underscored advancing theorem proving and research automation [7].

cited_items.title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
They Spent Years on a Math Problem. Then They Were Scooped by A.I. - The New York Times
OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
